2605.02442.title: Measuring AI Reasoning: A Guide for Researchers
2605.02442.paper_summary: In this paper, we offer a guide for researchers on evaluating reasoning in language models, building the case that reasoning should be assessed through evidence of adaptive, multi-step search rather than final-answer accuracy alone. Under an evaluation-oriented definition, reasoning requires selecting intermediate steps and halting according to input-dependent conditions, which we formalize as a search-like procedure. We show that single forward passes in scalable architectures are structurally limited in their ability to realize such variable-depth computation, motivating intermediate decoding and externalized reasoning traces as appropriate evaluation interfaces. Central to our argument is that final-answer accuracy alone is an insufficient measure of reasoning, because it provides little ability to diagnose or debug the underlying processes that produce individual solutions in frontier models. We therefore argue for a shift toward process-based evaluation, in which reasoning is assessed through the faithfulness and validity of intermediate reasoning traces as first-class evaluation targets.
2605.02442.paper_summary_zh: 在本文中，我們為研究人員提供了一個評估語言模型推理的指南，並建立了推理應該通過自適應的多步搜索證據來評估，而不僅僅是最終答案的準確性。根據以評估為導向的定義，推理需要根據依賴於輸入的條件選擇中間步驟並停止，我們將其形式化為一種類似搜索的程序。我們展示了在可擴展架構中單次前向傳遞在實現這種可變深度計算方面的結構性限制，這促使我們將中間解碼和外部推理痕跡視為合適的評估介面。我們論點的核心是，僅僅依賴最終答案的準確性並不足以衡量推理，因為它幾乎無法診斷或調試在前沿模型中產生個別解的底層過程。因此，我們主張向基於過程的評估轉變，在這種評估中，推理是通過中間推理痕跡的真實性和有效性作為一級評估目標來進行評估。
2605.02442.author: Munachiso Samuel Nwadike et.al.
2605.02442.authors: Munachiso Samuel Nwadike, Zangir Iklassov, Kareem Ali, Rifo Genadi, Kentaro Inui
